comment.body:
coding 5184.70 as the lvl where the puts start to print.
After AM expo happened, MMs will need to buyback a bit. Not too much. 
However, dealers are supplied with net short delta today, due to puts.
If puts are decaying, dealers will become longer delta.
If puts start to print, they become shorter delta. 
So this is "kinda supportive", not a bounce, but supportive in terms of not favoring for black swan, except if some very bad news occurs.

Post OpEx we can get some buyback flows, but after vol is coming back. 
Me personally, I don't like to bet on this upside move, rather short vol, but scaling out, and wait.
Still macro drive nenvironment as I keep saying

As sad as it sounds, since 2020 I have lost track of what is a good hedge for inflation.  It was supposed to be TIPS, then it wasn’t.  It was supposed to be hard assets/commodities, then it wasn’t.  It was supposed to be real estate/REITs, then it wasn’t.  It was supposed to be BTC and tech, then it wasn’t.  I view BTC as a hedge against being out of favor with your government more than an inflation hedge.  Maybe that will change over time.  

All that being said, I like gold and I try to add a little to my physical gold over time.  That is my dollar hedge.  So maybe through that lens it is a better inflation hedge.

comment.body:
I think alot of this confusion comes from not separating “asset price inflation” vs “cpi inflation”. 

For example, guys like Peter Schiff in 2009 were right on the call that inflation would be rampant over the next decades. However, it was “asset price inflation” that was rampant, not “CPI” inflation. So Schiff bought a bunch of precious metals, but really he should’ve loaded up on assets like tech, real estate, Bitcoin, etc. So the right call, but he wrong action.

comment.body:
coding 5184.70 as the lvl where the puts start to print.
After AM expo happened, MMs will need to buyback a bit. Not too much. 
However, dealers are supplied with net short delta today, due to puts.
If puts are decaying, dealers will become longer delta.
If puts start to print, they become shorter delta. 
So this is "kinda supportive", not a bounce, but supportive in terms of not favoring for black swan, except if some very bad news occurs.

Post OpEx we can get some buyback flows, but after vol is coming back. 
Me personally, I don't like to bet on this upside move, rather short vol, but scaling out, and wait.
Still macro drive nenvironment as I keep saying

comment.body:
Legally, Trump cannot fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell directly. The Federal Reserve operates as an independent agency, and its governors, including the chair, can only be removed "for cause," which typically means misconduct or malfeasance—not policy disagreements. Powell himself has emphasized that the Fed's independence is protected by law.

However, Trump has expressed frustration with Powell's decisions, particularly regarding interest rates and economic policies. There is a Supreme Court case that could potentially alter the legal landscape for independent agencies, but Powell has stated he doesn't believe it would apply to the Fed.
